title: Add playground command for Gemini ACP via Go ADK
description:
Goal:
Add an experimental `norma playground gemini-acp` workflow for exploring agentic CLI behavior by running Gemini CLI in ACP mode and connecting to it through Go ADK.

Acceptance:
- [ ] `norma playground gemini-acp --prompt "..."` supports a one-shot ACP-backed request.
- [ ] `norma playground gemini-acp` starts a terminal chat loop that reuses one ADK session for the invocation.
- [ ] Gemini CLI is spawned as `gemini --experimental-acp` and wrapped as an ADK `agent.Agent` through a reusable internal bridge.
- [ ] The implementation stays under `playground` only and does not change stable provider/config behavior.
- [ ] Automated tests cover command wiring, ACP transport behavior, ADK event mapping, and a fake ACP subprocess integration path.

Notes/Constraints:
- V1 is text-first: render text responses, surface richer ACP payloads only through debug logs.
- Reuse existing ADK runner/session patterns in the repo instead of inventing a parallel runtime.
- Keep `.norma/config.yaml` and provider selection unchanged for this experiment.
- Relevant references: Gemini CLI ACP support is experimental; current repo ADK usage already supports custom `agent.Agent` implementations and A2A, but not ACP client wiring.
close_reason: Implemented Gemini ACP playground command, reusable ACP bridge, ADK integration, tests, and docs.

title: Playground Gemini ACP: migrate to coder/acp-go-sdk + default zerolog diagnostics
description:
Goal:
Migrate the playground Gemini ACP stack from the custom ACP protocol/transport implementation to `github.com/coder/acp-go-sdk` and enable strict, default-on zerolog diagnostics for init, connection location, invocation lifecycle, and ACP transport flow.

Acceptance:
- [ ] `internal/adk/acpagent` no longer uses custom ACP JSON-RPC/protocol types; SDK (`acp-go-sdk`) is the transport and protocol source of truth.
- [ ] `internal/adk/acpagent/protocol.go` is removed and replaced by SDK-native ACP types in runtime paths.
- [ ] Playground diagnostics are enabled by default without requiring global `--debug` or playground-specific verbose flags.
- [ ] Lifecycle/session logs include command start, ACP process spawn context (where/cwd), session creation/reuse, prompt invocation boundaries, and errors.
- [ ] ACP wire traces (`send/recv` method + id/notification type) are emitted by default via zerolog.
- [ ] `norma playground gemini-acp` keeps text output UX unchanged for user-facing model responses.
- [ ] Existing behavior of one-shot and REPL session reuse remains correct.
- [ ] Tests are updated for SDK types and logging behavior; removed/legacy flags are covered.
- [ ] Quality gates pass: `go test ./...` and `go tool golangci-lint run`.
- [ ] No README/docs updates are made for this playground-only change.

Notes/Constraints:
- Hard replace package internals with SDK-native types and APIs.
- Keep scope constrained to playground and `internal/adk/acpagent`.
- Preserve ADK integration behavior (partial + final events, session reuse mapping).
notes:
Locked spec (must preserve):

Plan A — default zerolog diagnostics for playground gemini-acp:
- Remove `--verbose` and `--debug-events` toggles from playground path.
- Enable diagnostics by default for playground runtime.
- Log init/lifecycle/session/invocation with zerolog:
  - command start + resolved argv
  - ACP process spawn context (`cwd`/where)
  - ACP agent creation start/success/failure
  - ADK runner/session creation
  - per-turn invocation start/end + errors
  - REPL lifecycle and permission request/outcome
- Emit ACP wire traces (`send/recv`, method/id/notification type) by default.
- Keep user-facing model text rendering behavior unchanged.
- No README/docs updates for this playground-focused change.

Plan B — hard replace ACP implementation with coder SDK:
- Introduce module `github.com/coder/acp-go-sdk v0.6.3`.
- Replace custom ACP protocol/transport internals in `internal/adk/acpagent` with SDK APIs and types.
- Remove `internal/adk/acpagent/protocol.go` custom message/type layer.
- Use SDK connection primitives and typed request/response methods for initialize/new-session/prompt/cancel behavior.
- Adapt permission callbacks to SDK request/response/outcome models.
- Adapt ADK bridge streaming extraction to SDK session update unions/content blocks.
- Preserve ADK session -> remote ACP session reuse semantics.
- Preserve one-shot and REPL behavior parity.
- Update tests under `internal/adk/acpagent` and `cmd/norma` for SDK flow and logging defaults.
- Validate with `go test ./...` and `go tool golangci-lint run`.

Execution policy:
- Strict order via blocks dependencies in this feature.
- Do not broaden scope outside playground + internal ACP adapter.
- Do not edit README/docs.

title: Refactor to ephemeral agents + structured wrapper
description:
Objective:
Implement ephemeral agent runtime semantics across planner and PDCA workflows.

Success Criteria:
- Agents are created by registry config with required working directory.
- Agents do not receive user prompt at creation time.
- Factory supports creation-time system instruction only.
- Structured I/O is handled by wrapper layer, not by base agent factory.
- Planner ACP path uses creation instruction only (no prompt rewrite wrapper).
- All command config loaders normalize alias types consistently.
- `norma init` default config avoids non-runnable PDCA model types.

Implementation Plan:
1) Agent factory API and behavior
- Change CreationRequest to create-time fields only.
- Remove create-time Prompt/InputSchema/OutputSchema/RunDir usage.
- Require WorkingDirectory; fail fast if empty.
- generic_exec constructor: unstructured by default, set run dir from WorkingDirectory.
- generic_acp constructor: require WorkingDirectory, pass system instruction and command/model.

2) Structured I/O wrapper
- Add wrapper in internal/agent for role-based structured input/output.
- Wrapper composes role prompt + mapped request into invocation payload.
- Wrapper maps output back to normalized AgentResponse JSON.
- Keep JSON extraction fallback behavior for noisy outputs.

3) Planner alignment
- Remove planner ACP prompt rewrite wrapper usage.
- Use creation-time SystemInstruction in planner ACP creation paths.

4) Config normalization consistency
- Centralize alias normalization helper in internal/config.
- Apply it in loop/run/plan/runs config loaders and existing main loader.

5) Init defaults
- Update init default config to avoid gemini_api in PDCA role assignments.
- Keep defaults runnable via exec/acp aliases.

6) Tests and verification
- Update/Create tests for factory create-time semantics.
- Add tests for structured wrapper mapping in/out.
- Update planner tests for instruction-only ACP path.
- Add/adjust loader tests for normalization parity.
- Run `go test ./... -count=1` and `go tool golangci-lint run`.

Out of Scope:
- New schema fields.
- Runtime-level support for PDCA roles directly on gemini_api.
notes: Implemented end-to-end. Key changes: (1) ephemeral creation API with required WorkingDirectory and creation-only SystemInstruction, (2) structured I/O wrapper moved to internal/agent and runner, (3) planner ACP paths now instruction-only creation, (4) centralized alias normalization in internal/config and wired into loaders, (5) init defaults updated to avoid gemini_api PDCA assignment, (6) exec factory now uses permissive object schemas for raw JSON passthrough. Verification: go test ./... -count=1 and go tool golangci-lint run (0 issues).

title: StructuredIO: classify schema validation errors and retry output validation failures
description:
Goal:
Make structured wrapper resilient to transient output JSON/schema failures while preserving strict schema guarantees.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Add consistent sentinel errors for input/output schema validation classes.
- [ ] Retry only output schema-validation failures.
- [ ] Exhausted retries return typed output schema sentinel.

title: Implement bounded retry for structured output schema validation failures
description:

Retry wrapped-agent execution when output schema validation fails.

- Retry trigger: ErrStructuredOutputSchemaValidation only.

- Add option WithOutputValidationRetries(int).
- Default retries: 1 (2 total attempts).
- Include attempt index in logs and final exhaustion log.

Artifact:
- internal/adk/structuredio/wrapper.go
- internal/adk/structuredio/wrapper_test.go

Verify:
- transient invalid->valid output passes on retry
- persistent invalid output fails after max retries with output sentinel

close_reason: Added test verifying PDCA runner wraps errors with %%w, ensuring errors.Is works through wrapped errors

title: Expand structuredio tests for input/output validation and retry matrix

- invalid input -> ErrStructuredInputSchemaValidation, no retry

- invalid output all attempts -> ErrStructuredOutputSchemaValidation + umbrella sentinel
- non-schema execution errors do not get schema sentinel

close_reason: Added TestRetryMatrix with 4 test cases covering: invalid input (no retry), invalid output then valid (retry success), invalid output all attempts (exhausted), non-schema errors (no schema sentinel)

title: Standardize Logging on zerolog
description: Ensure the project uses only zerolog for logging by documenting requirements and migrating all code to a single logging library.

title: Document Logging Policy
description: Specify zerolog-only requirements and usage guidance in project agent documentation.

title: Update agents logging requirements
description:
Objective: Add explicit zerolog-only requirements and usage guidance to the agent documentation.
Artifact: AGENTS.md
Verify:
- rg -n "zerolog" AGENTS.md
- rg -n "logrus|zap|slog|log\." AGENTS.md
Notes: Include a clear prohibition on other logging libraries and a pointer to any preferred wrapper or package path.

title: Migrate Code to zerolog
description: Replace any non-zerolog logging usage with zerolog across the Go codebase.

title: Audit non-zerolog logging usage
description:
Objective: Identify all non-zerolog logging imports and call sites to be migrated.
Artifact: reports/logging-audit.txt
Verify:
- rg -n "logrus|zap|slog|log\." --glob '!**/vendor/**' . > reports/logging-audit.txt
- test -s reports/logging-audit.txt
Notes: If no matches, record the audit as empty with a brief note in the file.

title: Replace non-zerolog loggers
description:
Objective: Update all identified logging call sites to use zerolog (directly or via the project logging wrapper).
Artifact: **/*.go
Verify:
- rg -n "logrus|zap|slog|log\." --glob '!**/vendor/**' .
- go test ./...
Notes: Prefer a single project logging entry point if one exists (e.g., internal/logging).

title: Centralize Logging Configuration
description: Ensure a single, consistent zerolog configuration is used across the project.

title: Add or update logging setup
description:
Objective: Provide a centralized zerolog configuration (level, format, outputs) used by all components.
Artifact: internal/logging/logging.go
Verify:
- rg -n "internal/logging" --glob '**/*.go' .
- go test ./...
Notes: If a logging package already exists, update it instead of creating a new one.

title: Verification Guardrails
description: Add checks to prevent reintroducing other logging libraries.

title: Add logging lint check
description:
Objective: Add a simple repository check or script to fail CI if non-zerolog logging imports appear.
Artifact: scripts/check-logging.sh
Verify:
- bash scripts/check-logging.sh
- rg -n "logrus|zap|slog|log\." --glob '!**/vendor/**' .
Notes: If the project uses make or go:generate, integrate the check in the existing workflow.

# Plan

## Ordered backlog
1) T1 (S) Ensure console log output orders `role=` first when present.
   - Objective: Configure zerolog console writer to place the `role` field first in contextual fields.
   - Artifact: `internal/logging/logging.go` (ConsoleWriter config)
   - Verify: `go test ./...`

## Next Slice (1 feature + 1 task)
- Feature: Log lines show `role=` first when present for operator scanning.
- Task: T1 (S) Configure zerolog ConsoleWriter field ordering so `role` is first among fields.

## Stop conditions
- ConsoleWriter cannot order fields as required without broader logging format changes.
- Requirement implies `role` must appear before timestamp/level (not supported by current console writer).
- Change requires touching multiple logging outputs beyond the console writer.

## Verification checklist
- `go test ./...`
- Spot-check a run log line to confirm `role=...` appears first among fields.

title: Implement normaloop replan handoff flow
description:
Objective:
Implement normaloop replan handoff flow.

Required behavior on decision=replan:
- read current task and blocked dependents
- create new replanning task under same parent as old task
- relate old and new tasks
- for each issue blocked by old task, add new task as blocker
- add "replan-needed" label to old task
- close old task with reason "wont do: replan needed"
- return handled non-pass outcome without hard loop failure for this path

Artifact:
- internal/agents/normaloop/task.go
- supporting orchestration helpers as needed

Verify:
- unit tests demonstrate replan handoff and no hard-stop regression for handled path

title: Codex ACP bridge: support ACP session mcpServers (stdio+http)
description:
Goal:
Implement ACP-compliant `session/new` MCP server intake for `codex-acp-bridge` so clients can provide per-session MCP servers through `params.mcpServers`.

Acceptance:
- [ ] `session/new` accepts `mcpServers` from ACP request payload and validates each server.
- [ ] Supported transports are exactly `stdio` and `http`.
- [ ] Unsupported transports (including `sse`) are rejected with an ACP invalid-params error.
- [ ] Session-provided MCP servers are applied to first-turn Codex tool invocation for that session.
- [ ] Existing `session/set_model` behavior is preserved (no regression).
- [ ] Tests and docs cover the new protocol behavior.

Notes/Constraints:
- Keep current ACP lifecycle behavior (`initialize`, `new_session`, `prompt`, `cancel`, `set_mode`, `set_model`).
- Keep backward compatibility when `mcpServers` is empty.
- This feature tracks implementation only; no code is changed in this planning update.
close_reason: Implemented: session/new accepts and validates mcpServers (stdio/http only, SSE rejected), applies to first-turn Codex tool, existing set_model preserved, tests and docs added

title: Move Planner ADK Runtime Under internal/agents (Hard Cutover + Remove Legacy LLM Planner)
description:
### Move Planner ADK Runtime Under `internal/agents` (Hard Cutover + Remove Legacy LLM Planner)

### Summary
Relocate planner ADK runtime code from `internal/planner` into a new `internal/agents/planner` package, switch all callers to the new package (no compatibility wrappers), and remove the unused legacy `LLMPlanner` implementation and its `llmtools` support code.

### Implementation Changes
1. Create a new planner agent package at `internal/agents/planner` containing the active planner runtime and runtime-owned helpers.
- Move `AgentPlanner` and `RunInteractive` flow from current planner package.
- Move planner-agent runtime dependencies that are currently coupled to it:
  - planner instruction text used for ACP planner sessions,
  - ACP permission handler for mutating tool-call rejection,
  - planner-run error normalization (`ErrHandledInTUI` + formatting),
  - run-dir helper logic (`newPlanRunDir` + random suffix generation),
  - planner TUI runtime model/event rendering currently used by the interactive session.
- Keep runtime input typed as `planner.Request` from `internal/planner` to preserve domain-model ownership there.

2. Hard-cut all planner command call sites to the new package.
- Update `cmd/norma/plan/agent.go` to construct/run planner from `internal/agents/planner`.
- Update `cmd/norma/plan/web.go` to pull `PlannerInstruction` and `PlannerPermissionHandler` from `internal/agents/planner`.
- Keep `cmd/norma/plan/tui.go` request construction (`planner.Request`, `planner.ModeWizard`) in `internal/planner`.

3. Remove legacy planner implementation now.
- Delete legacy `LLMPlanner` code and helpers from `internal/planner` (including prompt/parsing code that only served that path).
- Delete `internal/planner/llmtools` package and its tests.
- Remove/replace tests tied only to deleted legacy behavior.

4. Re-home tests to match the new ownership.
- Move permission-handler tests to `internal/agents/planner`.
- Move TUI/interactive runtime tests to `internal/agents/planner`.
- Keep `internal/planner` tests only for retained domain/backlog pieces (`models`, `beads_tool`).

### Public/API Interface Changes
- `NewAgentPlanner`, `PlannerInstruction`, `PlannerPermissionHandler`, and `ErrHandledInTUI` move from `internal/planner` to `internal/agents/planner`.
- `internal/planner` remains the home for planning domain models (`Request`, decomposition models) and Beads-apply utility logic.
- No compatibility wrappers will be kept at old symbol locations.

### Test Plan
1. `go test ./...`
2. `go tool golangci-lint run`
3. Smoke check planner commands:
- `norma plan tui` starts interactive session and exits cleanly.
- `norma plan web` launches with planner agent using new package wiring.

### Assumptions
- Hard cutover is acceptable for all internal imports in this repo.
- Removing legacy `LLMPlanner` includes removing its `llmtools` package and related tests/docs references where they become stale.
- No external module imports these internal paths, so compile-time fixups in this repo are sufficient.
close_reason: Completed Move Planner ADK Runtime Under internal/agents (Hard Cutover + Remove Legacy LLM Planner)
